title: carex tenuis var. interjecta
intro: Carex tenuis var. interjecta, also known as Wood Sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. To utilize the health benefits of Wood Sedge, it can be prepared as a tea by infusing the dried leaves and roots in hot water, or as a tincture by soaking the plant in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
